questions.question: Describe the main difference between IPv4 and IPv6 address formats. (8 marks)
questions.hint: IPv4 uses a 32-bit address, while IPv6 uses a 128-bit address.

questions.question: A network device sends a packet to a destination IP address. What protocol(s) are used for routing the packet to its final destination? (6 marks)
questions.hint: Routing using Internet Control Message Protocol (ICMP) and Internet Protocol (IP)

questions.question: Explain the process of Fragmentation in packet switching. (10 marks)
questions.hint: Packet fragmentation occurs when a packet is too large to be transmitted over a network interface with a maximum transmission unit (MTU). The packet is divided into smaller fragments, each with a header and a portion of the original data.
